device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

conclusion.root_cause: Layer 0 activation outlier channels (absmax=166.75, 10x other layers) cause absmax INT4 to noise-ify mid-range values. INT3 zeros them instead, which is paradoxically less harmful for outlier-dominated attention.
conclusion.mechanism: absmax per-row quantization with extreme outliers: INT4 (qmax=7, scale=23.8) maps mid-range values (5-20) to noisy non-zero levels. INT3 (qmax=3, scale=55.6) maps the same values to zero. In attention where outlier channels dominate QK dot products, noisy mid-range is worse than clean zero.
conclusion.affected_layers: L0 (absmax=166.75, ΔPPL_int4=+179.4 vs ΔPPL_int3=+1.47) and L27 (absmax=408.75, outlier%=1.36%). Middle layers are normal.
conclusion.pythia_control: Pythia-6.9B shows monotonic bit→ΔPPL. Problem is Qwen-specific.
conclusion.connection_to_literature: Same phenomenon as SmoothQuant / LLM.int8() — activation outliers break naive absmax quantization.
conclusion.implication_for_arc: Arc prior (norm separation) and outlier-aware quantization are complementary. Combining both could address Qwen-type models.

part_a_synthetic.verdict: qa() is correct — MSE monotonically decreases with more bits on synthetic data

part_b_distribution.verdict: Qwen2-7B has massive outliers in L0 (absmax=166.75) and L27 (absmax=408.75). Middle layers have absmax=10-20.

part_c_per_head.verdict: MSE is monotonic (INT4 < INT3) for all heads. Reconstruction quality is not the issue.

part_d_per_layer.verdict: Layer 0 alone causes 99%+ of INT4 degradation. Other layers show INT4 ≈ INT3.
part_d_per_layer.layer_8_to_27: all < 0.3, ratios 0.4-0.9 (INT4 slightly better as expected)

part_e_pythia_control.verdict: Pythia-6.9B shows perfectly monotonic bit→ΔPPL. Problem is Qwen-specific.
